steps.title: Identify the transition relationship needed
steps.content: After claiming Chaucer helped pioneer literary English, the second sentence provides specific evidence of this (2,000+ first documented word uses). The transition must signal that this evidence reinforces the previous claim.

steps.title: Apply to the answer choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is confirmation: the second sentence provides concrete evidence that proves the claim about Chaucer pioneering literary English. The blank needs a transition word signaling that the following evidence affirms what was just stated.
  • Choice A — That being said,: signals a concession or qualification, so it fails because the second sentence strengthens, not limits, the claim. ✘
  • Choice B — Indeed,: signals that confirming evidence follows, so it fits — the documented first uses prove Chaucer's pioneering role. ✓
  • Choice C — Besides,: signals an additional separate point, so it fails because the manuscript evidence directly supports the pioneer claim rather than adding a new one. ✘
  • Choice D — However,: signals contrast or contradiction, so it fails because the second sentence agrees with the first. ✘
That matches Choice B — Indeed,.
